previous_statement: (Corollary: BP for AOU spaces) Let (V, V^+, e) be a finite-dimensional Archimedean order unit space and α_1, ..., α_m ∈ V*. For C = {x ∈ V^+ : α_j(x) = b_j}, any extreme point x of C satisfies dim(face_{V^+}(x)) ≤ m - 1. Proof: The Archimedean property implies V^+ is closed and pointed. In finite dimensions every face is finite-dimensional. Apply Theorem 1 (node 1).
new_statement: (Corollary: BP for AOU spaces) Let (V, V^+, e) be a finite-dimensional Archimedean order unit space (which by definition has pointed cone) and α_1, ..., α_m ∈ V*. For C = {x ∈ V^+ : α_j(x) = b_j}, any extreme point x of C satisfies d_F = dim(span(face_{V^+}(x))) ≤ m. Proof: By definition, an AOU space has a pointed cone. The Archimedean property implies V^+ is closed in the order-unit norm. In finite dimensions every face is finite-dimensional. Apply Theorem (node 1).
